name: pi05_libero
hw_notes: lm hidden_size=2048, head_dim=256, intermediate=16384 all multiples of 64: no padding
prefix seq = 3*256 (image) + 200 (text) = 968 tokens; padded to 1024 (16*64) for alignment; pad mask via attention bias, same mechanism as flash_attention_test seq-padding

vision head_dim=72 is NOT a multiple of 64: pad to 128 for flash attention, unpad via selection matrix on output (proven pattern from Swin 32->64 and MoonViT head_dim=72 -- same exact dim recurs here)
no RoPE anywhere in this model (pi0.5 uses AdaRMSNorm + sincos position embed for vision, absolute learned pos-embed for vision, no rotary at all in either LLM stack)

attention mask is NOT pure causal: prefix tokens attend bidirectionally to each other; suffix (action-expert) tokens attend causally relative to prefix + bidirectionally within the action block -- requires a precomputed static BIAS_DRAM tensor per flash_attention_core call, not the causal=true/false flag alone

SECOND LM STACK NOT REPRESENTABLE IN THIS SCHEMA: action-expert (Gemma-300M: 18 layers, hidden=1024, heads=8, kv_heads=1, head_dim=256, intermediate=4096) is a separate weight set sharing attention geometry with the prefix LM but run in a flow-matching denoising loop (x10, cached prefix KV reused). This manifest's lm block covers ONLY the prefix (Gemma-2B). The action-expert stack, AdaRMSNorm conditioning (matmul cond->3*hidden then split scale/shift/gate), sinusoidal timestep embedding (10 precomputed values, dt=-0.1 fixed schedule), and Euler integration loop are all hand-written deltas in pi05_libero_test.py -- not covered by generator flags.
output head is NOT a vocab/logit head: action_out_proj is a plain (1024,32) linear producing continuous 7-dim x 10-timestep action values, no softmax/argmax

quant note: matmat_mul_quantized_weights_test at IF4 on shape (M=968,K=2048,N=16384) passed 53-54dB SNR in isolation on real hw this session; failed catastrophically (-2.97dB) when chained after other quantized-weight test allocations in the same process -- suspected DRAM allocator overlap bug in test harness sequencing, not a model/op issue. Re-verify in isolation before trusting any IF4 numbers for this model; consider bf16 for first bring-up pass to de-risk.
